description: Perfect response for a biology topic with high confidence

content.description: A unique fact is: Tardigrades can survive the vacuum of outer space, withstanding extreme radiation, temperatures from -272°C to 150°C, and pressures six times greater than the deepest ocean trench.

metadata.reasoning: Confirmed by ESA's TARDIS experiment (2007) which exposed tardigrades to open space on the FOTON-M3 mission. Multiple peer-reviewed studies confirm their cryptobiosis capabilities.

trajectory.detail: Identified tardigrades' extreme survival as their most remarkable lesser-known property
Combined multiple survival extremes into a single concise fact
Cross-checked temperature range, pressure tolerance, and space exposure claims
